region_of_origin: Philippines (Jollibee brand); pineapple juice is globally produced

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pineapple juice entry; the canned/packaged commercial variant served at Jollibee Singapore is not individually coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pineapple juice generically; the specific canned fast-food serving format is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229424001 covers pineapple juice as a substance; the canned commercial product is not individually coded. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: sugar (may be added depending on formulation)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard can of pineapple juice as served at Jollibee Singapore, approximately 330 ml (330 g), consumed as a beverage with a meal.

nutrient_source.title: Pineapple juice, canned or bottled, unsweetened, without added ascorbic acid — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 09273)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for unsweetened canned pineapple juice used as the primary nutrient reference. The Jollibee-served product may contain added sugar; values represent the unsweetened canned baseline. Sugar and energy values may be slightly lower than the sweetened commercial variant. Adaptation noted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; pineapple juice, unsweetened, canned (GI=46)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) for unsweetened canned pineapple juice. The Jollibee-served canned product is treated as analogous to unsweetened canned pineapple juice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 12.87 × 3.30) / 100 ≈ 19.6 g GL for a 330 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); moderate potassium (130 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Canned pineapple juice is very low in sodium and provides modest potassium, making it generally acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, though portion size should be monitored due to natural sugar content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (9.98 g per 100 g) and moderate glycemic index (GI 46); a 330 ml can delivers approximately 33 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~19.6
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit canned pineapple juice due to its concentrated sugar content and moderate glycemic load; a full 330 ml can may cause a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be counted within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.01 g per 100 g) and very low sodium; contains vitamin C and potassium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Canned pineapple juice is very low in saturated fat and sodium, and provides antioxidant vitamin C, making it a low cardiovascular risk beverage when consumed in moderate portions as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pineapple Juice (Canned) is a commercially processed fruit beverage made from pressed and pasteurised pineapple juice, typically packaged in a sealed aluminium can of approximately 330 ml. At Jollibee Singapore (Bugis Village outlet), it is served as a cold beverage accompaniment to fast-food meals such as fried chicken and burgers. The juice has a bright golden-yellow colour, a sweet-tart tropical flavour, and a smooth, thin consistency without pulp. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 53 kcal, 12.87 g of carbohydrate (predominantly sugars at ~9.98 g), 0.36 g of protein, and 0.12 g of fat. It is a source of vitamin C (10.3 mg per 100 g), potassium (130 mg), and folate (15 mcg DFE). Sodium content is very low at 2 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 46 (low GI category), but a full 330 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 19.6, which is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The product is halal-certified in the Jollibee Singapore context, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It contains no significant fat or cholesterol. Canned pineapple juice is widely consumed across Singapore, Malaysia, and Southeast Asia as a refreshing tropical drink and is a standard fast-food beverage option.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of pineapple juice from Jollibee?
Is pineapple juice from Jollibee halal?
What is the sugar content of canned pineapple juice?
Is canned pineapple juice good for diabetics?
What is the GI of pineapple juice?
How much carbohydrate in a 330ml can of pineapple juice?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA SR Legacy for unsweetened canned pineapple juice (FDC ID 09273), which is the closest approved-source match. The exact Jollibee-branded canned product formulation is not available in an approved primary database; the product may contain added sugar, which would increase energy and sugar values slightly. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables for canned unsweetened pineapple juice. Confidence is medium due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: 1. The input food name 'Pineapple J Can (D)' is interpreted as 'Pineapple Juice Can (Drink)' as served at Jollibee Singapore. The canonical food name has been normalised to 'Pineapple Juice (Canned)'. 2. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 09273 (unsweetened canned pineapple juice per 100 g). The Jollibee product may be a sweetened commercial variant; actual sugar and energy may be marginally higher. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source for this product category; naturally zero as fruit juice contains no industrially produced trans fats. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived fruit juice with no animal-derived components. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as pineapple juice is a plant-based product containing no vitamin B12. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as pineapple juice is not a source of vitamin D and none is added in the standard canned product. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.01), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.04), and all remaining micronutrients are reported as per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 09273. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (46) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (12.87 g per 100 g × 3.30 serving factor) / 100 = 46 × 42.47 / 100 ≈ 19.5, rounded to 19.6. 9. The food is listed under 'Fast Food' cuisine in the input; this is preserved as the cuisine context (Jollibee fast-food chain), while region_of_origin reflects the beverage's broader tropical origin. 10. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean fast-food context: Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are mandatory per G6 rules for Singapore-served dishes. 11. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 09273 for this food item; however, all such fields have been populated with actual reported values from the USDA source where available. No -1 values remain in the final output as the USDA source provided comprehensive nutrient data for this food.
